what_it_does: This file is the living module constitution and drift-home for the skills registry: it defines a skill as a self-registering, file-discovered unit of reusable instructions, documents the skill:// address scheme, lists the current atomic and composition skills, explains how skill://<id> resolves, and states the rules for adding or removing skills.
observations: Skills are declared one per file in skills/<id>.py as a module-level SKILL dict with minimal schema {id, content, label, description}.
skill://<id> resolves to the skill's content via runtime/skills.py:SkillRegistry.read, dispatched by runtime/cognition.py:resolve_address.
The live atomic skills listed are summarize and extract_decisions.
Four composition skills are listed as multi-step workflow recipes: corpus_pipeline, patterned_visibility, inversion_query, and map_reduce_composition.
Composition skills name real tools from mcp_face/server.py but only describe how to compose them; they do not encode dispatch/approve/launch logic.
The file explicitly flags two tool-surface gaps: no standalone embed MCP tool and no write-mark tool.
Registry-is-truth is enforced: an unknown skill id raises fail-loud rather than fabricating.
The registry mirrors the self-registration patterns used by roles and node-types.
tests/skills_contexts_acceptance.py fails if a discovered skill is not reflected in this drift home.
standouts: Skills are file-discovered and self-registering exactly like roles and node-types, with skill:// as the address seam.
Composition skills encode multi-step cognition workflows that a single tool description cannot capture.
extract_decisions is presented as proof of agent-authored skill creation via create_skill, committed [self-apply] without surfaced approval.
Resolution of an unknown skill id is explicitly fail-loud.
conventions: Follows the constitution format with YAML front matter, status: living, and a drift-home structure.
Follows the registry-is-truth principle: the directory is the source of truth, not a hardcoded list.
Reuses the same registry pattern as roles and nodes rather than inventing a new one.
Extends the address scheme additively through contracts/address.py:SCHEMES.
Requires the drift home to be updated whenever a skill file is added.
concerns: The composition recipes reference many external tool names that are not defined in this file, so the file's accuracy depends on those tools remaining stable elsewhere.
The acceptance test guards reflection but still requires a manual update of this markdown file, creating a possible drift point.
The file honestly notes missing standalone embed and write-mark tools, which means the recipes currently rely on engine-side behavior or workarounds.
notes: This is the living constitution and drift-home for the file-discovered skill registry, cataloging the current skill set and the rules for adding and resolving skill:// addresses.
questions: What exact validation does SkillRegistry.apply perform when a SKILL dict is malformed or its id does not match the file stem?
How does the context:// scheme differ from skill:// in practice, given that contexts are described as the same shape?
Are composition skills intended to be read directly by roles as input, or are they primarily operator/agent playbooks for composing tools?
What does 'LAYER-2 embed is the engine pass over embeddable lenses' mean operationally?
Is there a programmatic way to enforce that the drift home is updated before a skill file change is considered complete?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file states it is the skills module constitution and drift home, and it documents the registry, live skills, resolution path, and update rules.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish reusable role instructions as first-class, addressable registry entries and to provide declarative, operator-facing recipes for multi-step cognition workflows without baking in dispatch or approval logic.
novelty: It treats instructions as registry entries resolvable at skill://, and it introduces composition skills that are declarative multi-step recipes naming real tools rather than single tool descriptions.
contribution: A documented, file-discovered skill registry pattern plus a catalog of reusable instruction units and composition recipes that a fused system can keep as the canonical description of skill:// semantics and the live skill set.
summary_for_embedding: The living constitution for the file-discovered skill registry, defining skills as self-registering instruction files addressable at skill://<id>, listing the current skills and composition recipes, and specifying resolution and update rules.
intention_for_embedding: To make reusable role instructions and multi-step cognition workflows discoverable, addressable, and maintainable as a registry-as-truth module.
